itineraries.name: Porto and Gaia Port weekend
itineraries.theme: Two days of Port lodges, riverside wine bars and museums in Porto and Vila Nova de Gaia

days.morning: Cross the Dom Luis I bridge to Vila Nova de Gaia and tour the Taylor's Port cellars, finishing in the peacock-dotted tasting garden with a flight of tawny and vintage Port.
days.lunch: Lunch on the Gaia waterfront before walking up the hill.
days.afternoon: Continue to Graham's 1890 Lodge for a guided tour and a tasting in the Vintage Room among bottles dating to the 1890s.
days.evening: Aperitif at the Espaco Porto Cruz 360 rooftop lounge over the river, then dinner in Porto.
days.accommodation: Stay at The Yeatman wine hotel on the Gaia hillside.

days.morning: Visit the WOW World of Wine cultural district in Gaia, taking in the wine museum and a self-guided tasting.
days.lunch: Lunch in Ribeira across the river.
days.afternoon: Taste through small-grower Ports at Vinologia in Ribeira, then a Douro-focused glass at Prova.
days.evening: Late candle-lit wine in the former chapel at Capela Incomum in Cedofeita.
days.accommodation: Second night at The Yeatman or a Porto centre hotel.

itineraries.description: A two-day Porto and Gaia weekend through the historic Port lodges, the WOW museum quarter and the riverside wine bars of Ribeira, with a wine-hotel base.
itineraries.tip: Book the Graham's Vintage Room and Taylor's cellar tours ahead; the lodges fill in high season.

itineraries.name: Cima Corgo quintas, three days
itineraries.theme: Into the heart of the Douro: Regua, Pinhao and the Cima Corgo quintas

days.morning: Drive or take the scenic train up to Peso da Regua and visit the Museu do Douro to ground the trip in the valley's history.
days.lunch: Lunch at Castas e Pratos by the Regua station.
days.afternoon: Tasting and classes at Quinta do Vallado just outside Regua, on the banks of the Corgo.
days.evening: Drive on to Pinhao and settle in along the river.
days.accommodation: Stay at Quinta do Vallado's wine hotel near Regua.

days.morning: Visit Quinta de la Rosa just outside Pinhao for a cellar tasting of its reds and Ports under winemaker Jorge Moreira.
days.lunch: Lunch on the Quinta de la Rosa terrace over the river.
days.afternoon: Continue to the Bomfim 1896 experience at Pinhao for a Symington tasting in the vineyards.
days.evening: Dinner at the historic Vintage House in Pinhao.
days.accommodation: Stay at Quinta de la Rosa or The Vintage House in Pinhao.

days.morning: Take a rabelo boat cruise from Pinhao to read the terraced landscape from the water.
days.afternoon: Finish with a visit to Wine and Soul at Vale Mendiz for its cult Pintas and Manoella, by appointment.
days.evening: Return downriver to Porto or Regua.

itineraries.description: A three-day route through the Cima Corgo from Regua to Pinhao, taking in the Douro museum, riverside quintas, a Symington vineyard tasting and the cult Wine and Soul estate.
itineraries.tip: Wine and Soul is by appointment only; arrange the visit before you set out, and take the train at least one way for the views.

itineraries.name: Douro harvest experience long weekend
itineraries.theme: September vindima: picking, foot-treading and harvest feasts in the Douro

days.morning: Begin at Quinta do Tedo near Folgosa for a tour of the organic estate at the meeting of the Tedo and Douro rivers.
days.lunch: Harvest lunch with the pickers at Quinta do Tedo.
days.afternoon: Join the afternoon foot-treading in the granite lagares as the day's grapes come in.
days.evening: Dinner at DOC by Rui Paula on the riverbank at Folgosa.

days.morning: Transfer to Pinhao for a full harvest day at Quinta da Pacheca, picking on the terraces.
days.lunch: Traditional harvest lunch at Quinta da Pacheca.
days.afternoon: Grape treading at Croft's Quinta da Roeda, the classic Port-house harvest experience.

days.morning: Ride the historic Douro steam train between Regua and Tua for a final look at the harvest-season terraces.
days.afternoon: Last tasting and a tasting class at Quinta do Vallado before departing.

itineraries.description: A three-day September vindima trip built around picking, granite-lagar foot-treading and harvest feasts at organic and Port-house quintas, with the historic Douro train.
itineraries.tip: Harvest dates shift with ripening; confirm picking and treading sessions with each quinta and book by late summer.

itineraries.name: Douro grand tour, five days
itineraries.theme: Porto, the Cima Corgo and the remote Douro Superior end to end
itineraries.base: Porto, Pinhao and the Douro Superior

days.morning: Start in Gaia with the Sandeman cellars and their bottle museum under the black-caped Don.
days.afternoon: Tour the WOW World of Wine quarter and its museum.
days.evening: Riverside wine bar evening at the Wine Quay Bar in Ribeira.

days.morning: Drive up to Lamego and tour the area, then on to a luxury base above the valley.
days.afternoon: Check in and taste at Six Senses Douro Valley near Lamego.
days.evening: Dinner at the resort overlooking the river.
days.accommodation: Stay at Six Senses Douro Valley.

days.morning: Visit Quinta de la Rosa near Pinhao for a tasting under Jorge Moreira.
days.lunch: Lunch on the Quinta de la Rosa terrace.
days.afternoon: Afternoon tasting at the Bomfim 1896 experience in Pinhao.
days.evening: Dinner at The Vintage House in Pinhao.
days.accommodation: Stay at The Vintage House, Pinhao.

days.morning: Push east into the remote Douro Superior toward Vale Meao country.
days.afternoon: Visit Quinta Nova Winery House near Covas do Douro for a cellar tasting.
days.evening: Dinner at the Conceitus Winery Restaurant.
days.accommodation: Stay at Quinta Nova Winery House.

days.morning: A final visit to the cult Wine and Soul estate at Vale Mendiz, by appointment.
days.afternoon: Take the scenic train back down the valley toward Porto.
days.evening: Final glass in Porto before departure.

itineraries.description: A five-day end-to-end Douro tour from the Gaia Port lodges through the Cima Corgo quintas to the remote Douro Superior, mixing museums, luxury stays and cult estates.
itineraries.tip: The Douro Superior is a long drive from Porto; build in time and book Wine and Soul and the better restaurants well ahead.
